description: Create the workflow_step_usage_periods table keyed by tenant, period_start, and period_end with fields for period source, effective limit, used count, limit source, tier, Stripe subscription id, metadata, and timestamps.
prdRefs: Data / API / Integrations > New enforcement table

description: Add indexes and tenant isolation/RLS support for workflow_step_usage_periods consistent with workflow runtime tables.
prdRefs: Data / API / Integrations > New enforcement table

description: Implement a quota resolver that selects the tenant's active Stripe subscription period when available.
Implement fallback quota periods using the current UTC calendar month when no valid active Stripe subscription period exists.
Resolve tenant tier defaults for workflow step limits: solo 150, pro 750, and premium 10000.
Read workflow_step_limit from Stripe price metadata and use it as the highest-precedence finite or unlimited limit override.
Read workflow_step_limit from Stripe product metadata as a fallback override when price metadata is missing or invalid.
Treat workflow_step_limit=unlimited as effective_limit null while continuing to record used_count.
Ignore invalid workflow_step_limit metadata and fall back to the next valid source without crashing workflow execution.
Implement an atomic quota reservation service that upserts and locks the tenant period row before incrementing used_count.
prdRefs: Data / API / Integrations > New enforcement table

description: Reject finite quota reservations without incrementing used_count when used_count is already at or above effective_limit.
Allow unlimited quota reservations to increment used_count and continue execution regardless of current count.
Integrate quota reservation into WorkflowRuntimeV2 before workflow_run_steps STARTED rows are created.
prdRefs: Data / API / Integrations > Runtime integration points

description: Integrate quota reservation into projectWorkflowRuntimeV2StepStart before Temporal workflow_run_steps STARTED rows are created.
prdRefs: Data / API / Integrations > Runtime integration points

description: Create or reuse a workflow_run_waits record with wait_type quota when a reservation is rejected for quota exhaustion.
prdRefs: Data / API / Integrations > Quota wait payload

description: Mark quota-exhausted runs WAITING at the current node_path without creating a workflow_run_steps row for the blocked step.
Ensure quota pauses are treated as controlled waits rather than workflow failures, retryable step errors, or auto-pause failure-rate inputs.
Log quota reservation, quota exhaustion, invalid metadata fallback, and fallback-calendar usage with structured workflow/runtime context.
Implement a scheduled workflow-quota-resume-scan job that finds WAITING quota waits and resumes eligible runs.
Add locking and batch limits to the quota resume scan so concurrent scans do not resolve the same waits.
Resume at most the finite remaining capacity for a tenant during each scan while relying on runtime reservation for final enforcement.
prdRefs: Users and Primary Flows > Background scheduler

description: Add a manual resume action for quota-paused workflow runs that verifies tenant ownership, permissions, and quota eligibility.
prdRefs: Data / API / Integrations > Manual resume API/action

description: Return a helpful exhausted-quota response from manual resume when quota is still unavailable, including usage, limit, and reset time.
Trigger or enqueue normal workflow execution after an automatic or manual quota resume without pre-consuming quota.
Expose minimal run-level quota pause information through existing run logs, wait records, or inspector surfaces.
Implement a reconciliation helper that compares workflow_step_usage_periods.used_count to workflow_run_steps joined through workflow_runs for the same tenant and period.
Ensure workflow retries consume a new step unit only when the retry step attempt starts.
Ensure forEach body executions consume one step unit per item attempt.
Ensure wait and human task steps consume one step unit when first entered and do not double-count while merely waiting.
Document quota source, limits, pause behavior, resume behavior, and reconciliation procedure for future support and engineering use.
